title: Sora's Constellation Map
content: Sora maintains a hand-annotated constellation map of Springtown's night sky. It records official constellations, meteor shower dates, local viewing points, and small personal notes from seven years of observation journals. She marks nights when Luna visited, nights when clouds broke unexpectedly, and nights when children saw Saturn's rings for the first time. The map is scientific, but it also proves that wonder can be documented without being reduced.
